allowed_source_ips.description: An array of allowed source IP ranges (in CIDR notation) that are permitted to access the API. This helps secure the API by ensuring only trusted IPs can communicate with it. For more details on CIDR notation, visit the [CIDR Documentation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classless_Inter-Domain_Routing).
allowed_source_ips.title: Allowed Source IP Ranges

api_id.description: A unique identifier for the API, used for API-specific logging, monitoring, and identification in ProxyConf and Envoyproxy. This ID is essential for tracking and debugging API traffic across the system.
api_id.title: API Identifier

authentication.description: The auth object handles authentication for both downstream and upstream requests. This allows you to specify client authentication requirements for incoming requests and credential injection for outgoing requests to upstream services.
authentication.title: Authentication

cidr.description: The IP address range in CIDR notation.
cidr.title: IP Address Range

cluster.description: The cluster identifier groups APIs for Envoy. This cluster name should also be reflected in the static `bootstrap` configuration of Envoy, ensuring that APIs are properly associated with the correct Envoy instances.
cluster.title: Cluster Identifier

disabled.description: Disabling any downstream authentication. This potentially allows untrusted traffic. It's recommended to further limit exposure by narrowing the `allowed-source-ips` as much as possible.
disabled.title: Disabled

downstream_auth.description: Configuration for downstream client authentication. This typically involves specifying authentication types (e.g., API keys) and client credentials.
downstream_auth.title: Downstream Authentication

fail_fast_on_missing_header_parameter.description: Reject requests that are missing required headers as defined in the OpenAPI spec. You can override this setting at the path level using the `x-proxyconf-fail-fast-on-missing-header-parameter` field in the OpenAPI path definition.
fail_fast_on_missing_header_parameter.title: Fail Fast on Missing Header Parameter

fail_fast_on_missing_query_parameter.description: Reject requests that are missing required query parameters. Similar to headers, this setting can be overridden at the path level with the `x-proxyconf-fail-fast-on-missing-query-parameter` field.
fail_fast_on_missing_query_parameter.title: Fail Fast on Missing Query Parameter

fail_fast_on_wrong_media_type.description: Reject requests where the `content-type` header doesn't match the media types specified in the OpenAPI request body spec. You can override this behavior at the path level using the `x-proxyconf-fail-fast-on-wrong-media-type` field.
fail_fast_on_wrong_media_type.title: Fail Fast on Wrong Media Type

ip_address.description: The IP address Envoy listens for new TCP connections
ip_address.title: Listener Address

mtls.description: Enabling mutual TLS for all clients that access this API. The `subject` or `SAN` in the provided client certificate is matched against the list provided in the `clients` property.
mtls.title: Mutual TLS

mtls_clients.description: The clients are matches based on the client certificate subject or SAN
mtls_clients.title: Allowed Clients

mtls_subjects.description: The clients are matches based on the client certificate subject or SAN
mtls_subjects.title: Certificate Subject / SubjectAlternativeName (SAN)

mtls_type.description: Constant `mtls` identifiying that mutual TLS is used for authenticating downstream HTTP requests.
mtls_type.title: Authentication Type

proxyconf.description: The `x-proxyconf` property extends the OpenAPI specification with ProxyConf-specific configurations, enabling ProxyConf to generate the necessary resources to integrate with [Envoyproxy](https://www.envoyproxy.io/).
proxyconf.title: ProxyConf API Config

security.description: The `security` object configures API-specific security features, such as IP filtering and authentication mechanisms. It supports both source IP filtering (allowing only specific IP ranges) and client authentication for downstream requests, as well as credential injection for upstream requests.
security.title: Security Configuration

tcp_port.description: The port is extracted from the `api_url` if it is explicitely provided as part of the url. E.g. the implicit ports 80/443 for http/https are replaced by the default `8080`.
tcp_port.title: Listener Port

trusted_ca.description: A path to a PEM encoded file containing the trusted CAs. This file must be readable by the ProxyConf server and is automatically distributed to the Envoy instances using the SDS mechanism
trusted_ca.title: Trusted Certificate Authority (CA)

url.description:
The API URL serves multiple functions:
- **Scheme**: Determines if TLS or non-TLS listeners are used (e.g., `http` or `https`).
- **Domain**: Used for virtual host matching in Envoy.
- **Path**: Configures prefix matching in Envoy's filter chain.
- **Port**: If specified, this overrides the default listener port. Ensure you explicitly configure HTTP ports `80` and `443`.
url.title: API URL

Elixir.ProxyConf.ConfigGenerator.Downstr.description: The `downstream` object configures the authentication mechanism applied to downstream HTTP requests. Defining an authentication mechanism is required, but can be opted-out by explicitely configuring `disabled`.
Elixir.ProxyConf.ConfigGenerator.Downstr.title: Downstream Authentication

Elixir.ProxyConf.ConfigGenerator.Listene.description: The `listener` object configures the Envoy listener used to serve this API. Depending on the provided `api_url` a TLS context is configured.
Elixir.ProxyConf.ConfigGenerator.Listene.title: Listener Configuration
